items.hook: Kurdistani Jews are the Mizrahi Jewish communities from the geographic region of Kurdistan, roughly covering parts of northwestern Iran, northern Iraq, northeastern Syria and southeastern Turkey.
items.body: Kurdistani Jews lived as closed ethnic communities until they were expelled from Kurdistan, as part of the wider expulsion of Jews from Arab and Muslim states in the 1940s–1950s. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.body: The jubilee edition will unspool under the motto “Show up or it doesn't count!” and feature 105 films across eight competition sections

items.hook: The Amerika Haus Berlin is an institution that was developed following the end of the World War II, to provide an opportunity for German citizens to learn more about American culture and politics, and engage in discussion and debate on the transatlantic relationship.
items.body: Run by the American government until 2006, Berlin's Amerika Haus is one of many Amerika Häuser located across Germany. The world is stranger than it lets on.
